channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: WATU Credit's Smartphone Financing Program Boosts Digital Inclusion in Tanzania
item.description: WATU Credit, a Tanzanian smartphone financing company, has achieved a milestone of serving over one million customers through its affordable device financing service. Launched five years ago, the prog...

item.title: Suspect arrested in Basilan couple's murder case
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) arrested a suspect in the murder of a couple in Basilan, Philippines. Rubber tappers Saturani Bancil and his wife Muhayyara Samsawi were shot in the head at a gras...

item.title: Centre Appoints New Chiefs for BPR&D, NCRB, and SVPNPA
item.description: The Indian government has appointed Alok Kumar Mittal, a 1993 batch IPS officer of Haryana cadre, as Director General of the Bureau of Police Research & Development (BPRD). Mittal, currently serving a...

item.title: Jasmine Paolini advances to Wimbledon quarter-finals after defeating Alexandra Eala
item.description: Italian 13th seed Jasmine Paolini secured her first Grand Slam quarter-final appearance since 2024 after a hard-fought three-set victory over rising star Alexandra Eala at Wimbledon. The match, played...

item.title: Analyst Highlights Strong REIT Performance and Future Outlook Amid Rising Returns
item.description: The article discusses the strong performance of Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) in Canada, driven by rising return expectations and analyst optimism. CIBC analyst Dean Wilkinson predicts continu...

item.title: Harry Styles breaks Guinness World Record with 12-show Wembley Stadium residency
item.description: Harry Styles has been officially recognised by Guinness World Records for his record-breaking 12-show residency at Wembley Stadium, surpassing the previous record held by Coldplay. The achievement was...

item.title: FG calls on media to curb spread of extremist narratives
item.description: The Federal Government has called on journalists and social media influencers to avoid giving publicity to bandits and terrorists, emphasizing the media's role in promoting unity and countering disinf...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Continues Hearings on Justice System Allegations
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, established to investigate allegations of criminality, political interference, and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, is proceeding with its ...

item.title: RADV Driver Enhancements Enable Performance Gains via VRS Optimizations
item.description: Marek Olšák, a prominent figure in AMD Linux graphics driver development, has made significant contributions to the RADV Vulkan driver, enhancing variable rate shading (VRS) code to achieve performanc...

item.title: Wimbledon Royal Box: Celebrities and Influencers Attend
item.description: The Wimbledon Championships witnessed a gathering of notable figures in the Royal Box, including Sir Lenny Henry, Billie Jean King, Alessia Russo, Mustafa Suleyman, and other prominent individuals. Si...

item.title: Footballer's fatal injury highlights risks of playing on covered cricket pitches
item.description: A 27-year-old schoolteacher and Australian rules footballer, Nathan Fitzgerald, died after sustaining critical head injuries during a game at Lalor Recreation Reserve in Melbourne. The incident occurr...

item.title: Humid Conditions and Scattered Showers Expected in London
item.description: The article discusses the upcoming weather forecast for London, highlighting the presence of clouds and scattered showers as humid conditions develop. Published on July 6, 2026, the piece provides an ...

item.title: Sam Larry Survives Lagos Road Accident, Associate Confirms Survival
item.description: Popular music promoter Samson Eletu, known as Sam Larry, is alive and receiving medical treatment after a severe road accident in Lagos State. His associate, Akin Abolade (Mr Lilgaga), confirmed his s...

item.title: Government Announces New Environmental Policies to Combat Climate Change
item.description: The government has unveiled a comprehensive set of environmental policies aimed at addressing climate change. These measures include stricter emissions regulations for industries, incentives for renew...

item.title: Conversion of Water Sources to Farms Threatens Kilombero's Water Security
item.description: Residents in Tanzania’s Kilombero district are increasingly converting water sources into agricultural land, a practice that threatens the region’s water security and environmental sustainability. The...

item.title: 27 Sheep and Goats Stolen from Limpopo Kraal
item.description: A livestock owner in Makotse, Limpopo, reported the theft of 27 animals (16 sheep and 11 goats) valued at approximately R63,600. The incident occurred between Friday evening and Saturday morning, with...

item.title: Bruce Springsteen Criticizes Trump, Sparks Outcry from MAGA Supporters
item.description: In a PBS special titled 'Bruce Springsteen: Finding America in Song,' the musician criticized President Donald Trump, calling him a 'coward' and advocating for 'critical patriotism.' Springsteen empha...

item.title: California's Ballot Measures: Key Propositions and Their Implications
item.description: California's upcoming ballot measures have sparked intense political and public debate, with multiple proposals addressing critical issues such as housing, healthcare, wealth taxation, and environment...

item.title: PCSO Major Lotto Games Have No Jackpot Winners on July 6, 2026
item.description: The Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) announced that no one won the jackpot prizes in its major lotto draws on July 6, 2026. The Grand Lotto 6/55 jackpot, valued at P63,065,055.92, remains ...

item.title: Trump urges FIFA to review US player's red card suspension amid Belgium's challenge
item.description: President Donald Trump has admitted calling FIFA president Gianni Infantino to request a review of a red card issued to US forward Folarin Balogun during the World Cup. The incident occurred after Bal...
